name: Starting Now Corporation
description_short: 501(c)(3) nonprofit credit counseling and housing agency serving underserved communities across South Florida and nationwide, offering free personalized financial guidance.
description_long:
Starting Now Corporation is a minority woman-led 501(c)(3) nonprofit founded in 2017, based in South Florida. The organization was established to address financial literacy gaps and provide accessible financial services to underserved populations including low-to-moderate-income individuals, minorities, immigrants, women, and veterans. Their mission explicitly focuses on empowering these communities to achieve financial stability and long-term economic success while breaking down barriers to access.

The organization provides four primary service areas: credit counseling (one-on-one personalized guidance for any credit situation), housing counseling (comprehensive support for potential homebuyers), homebuyer education (first-time buyer preparation), and general financial education. All services are delivered with free, friendly, no-obligation initial consultations. The agency operates a multilingual approach, offering services in Spanish, Polish, and ASL to better serve diverse communities. They also leverage partnerships to provide subsidized program enrollment, extending their reach beyond their South Florida base to serve clients nationwide.

Starting Now distinguishes itself through its explicit commitment to historically underserved populations and its status as a minority woman-led organization. Rather than offering credit repair or debt settlement (which can be predatory), they focus on education and personalized counseling—helping clients understand credit mechanics, navigate major life events, and prepare for significant financial decisions like homeownership. Their approach targets specific pain points: mortgage approval barriers, rental rejections, auto loan challenges, employment credit checks, business credit issues, and the particular stresses facing single parents rebuilding credit.

As a nonprofit with no apparent revenue-generation agenda, Starting Now appears genuinely focused on financial empowerment rather than profit. However, the website provides minimal specifics about counselor credentials (though the free-help category typically implies HUD approval or NFCC certification), success rates, or detailed program outcomes. Potential clients should verify whether counselors hold required certifications and understand what specific outcomes to expect from each service.
address: 3301 N University Dr, Coral Springs, FL 33065

company_info.headquarters: 3301 N University Dr, Coral Springs, FL 33065

pros: Explicitly serves underserved populations (low-income, minorities, immigrants, women, veterans) with culturally competent approach
Multilingual services available in Spanish, Polish, and ASL for accessibility
All initial consultations are free, friendly, and no-obligation with no hidden costs
Minority woman-led organization, embodying diversity values in leadership and operations
Nationwide reach through partnerships despite South Florida base, extending subsidized access beyond local area
Comprehensive service portfolio (credit counseling, housing counseling, homebuyer education, financial education) addressing multiple financial life stages
Addresses specific hardship scenarios (single parents rebuilding, major life events, employment credit checks) with targeted guidance
cons: Website does not explicitly state HUD approval or NFCC certification status, which is standard for nonprofit credit counseling agencies
No information provided about counselor qualifications, credentials, or experience levels
No success metrics, client outcomes, or satisfaction data published on website to demonstrate effectiveness
Website lacks details on program duration, time commitment, or what clients should realistically expect to achieve
Limited information about how 'subsidized program enrollment' through partnerships actually works or what it costs
best_for: Low-to-moderate-income individuals seeking free credit counseling without sales pressure or predatory tactics
First-time homebuyers preparing for mortgage approval and navigating the homeownership process
Single parents rebuilding credit while managing financial stress and family responsibilities
Immigrants, minorities, and non-English speakers needing culturally competent and multilingual financial guidance
diagnosis: Starting Now is best for financially vulnerable individuals—particularly from underserved communities—who need honest credit education and housing preparation without predatory terms or pressure to buy products. The main caveat is that the website lacks documentation of counselor credentials and program outcomes, so potential clients should independently verify HUD/NFCC certification and ask specific questions about what results to expect before committing time to counseling.
services: Homebuyer education and first-time buyer preparation
General financial education and literacy training
Services for major life event planning (marriage, divorce, etc.)
